title: Complex Tense Interactions in Narrative
description: Futuro en el pasado, presente histórico, y discurso indirecto libre. Los tiempos verbales como herramienta narrativa avanzada.

items.front: Complex Tense Interactions in Narrative (was going to / was about to / would later become (future in the past) | Historical present | Free indirect speech)
items.back: Interacciones complejas de tiempos verbales en narrativa: futuro en el pasado, presente histórico y discurso indirecto libre
items.explanation: En narrativa avanzada los tiempos verbales se combinan de maneras sofisticadas. (1) FUTURO EN EL PASADO: 'was going to', 'was about to', 'would later become' — describe lo que iba a suceder desde la perspectiva del pasado narrativo. (2) PRESENTE HISTÓRICO: narrar eventos pasados en presente para crear inmediatez — 'So I'm walking down the street and this guy comes up'. (3) DISCURSO INDIRECTO LIBRE: mezclar narración con pensamientos del personaje sin verbos de reporte — 'She walked to the door. Was this really happening?'. Usado por Austen, Woolf, Joyce. Uno de los elementos más difíciles de traducir.

cardRef.front: Complex Tense Interactions in Narrative (was going to / was about to / would later become (future in the past) | Historical present | Free indirect speech)

items.back: Little did she know that the letter she was about to open would change everything.
items.explanation: El 'futuro en el pasado' toma la perspectiva de un momento pasado y mira hacia lo que 'iba a suceder' desde allí. Tres formas principales: (1) 'was/were going to' (plan o predicción), (2) 'was/were about to' (inminencia), (3) 'would' + infinitivo (futuro narrativo). Común en biografía, novela y crónica histórica.

items.back: She stared at the ceiling. Why had he said that? What was she supposed to do now?
items.explanation: El discurso indirecto libre es una técnica literaria que difumina la frontera entre el narrador y el personaje. Se reconoce porque: (1) no tiene verbo de reporte ('she thought/wondered'), (2) mantiene el backshift de tiempos del indirecto (past perfect, past modal), (3) conserva la entonación subjetiva del personaje (preguntas retóricas, exclamaciones). Marca de prosa literaria sofisticada.

steps.title: Tiempos verbales como recurso narrativo
steps.instruction: En C2, los tiempos no solo marcan cuándo sucede algo — son herramientas estilísticas. Aprendé a reconocer y usar: futuro en el pasado, presente histórico y discurso indirecto libre.

steps.title: ¿Qué recurso narrativo?
steps.instruction: Identificá qué técnica narrativa usa cada oración.

steps.title: Elegí el uso correcto
steps.instruction: ¿En qué oración se usa correctamente el recurso narrativo?

exercises.translation: estaba por (futuro en el pasado, inminencia)
options: She was about to leave when the phone rang.
I was about to yesterday's meeting when I got lost.
He will be about to finish the project next week.
They are about to already finished the race.

exercises.translation: presente histórico (narrar pasado en presente para inmediatez)
options: Yesterday I go to the store and I buy some milk.
So there I am, standing in the rain, and suddenly she walks up and says hello.
Last week she will visit her grandmother every day.

exercises.translation: discurso indirecto libre (mezcla narración y pensamiento sin verbo de reporte)
options: She said that she was tired and wanted to go home.
He asked if the meeting had been postponed.
Sarah walked to the window. Had he really meant what he said? Could she trust him?

steps.title: Combinando tiempos narrativos
steps.instruction: Completá el pasaje con las formas verbales correctas.

exercises.template: As she stepped onto the platform, she had no idea that the next few minutes {0} change her life forever. She {1} about to board what would turn out to be the wrong train — a decision that {2} later become the subject of countless conversations with her therapist.

steps.title: ¡Lección completada!
steps.instruction: NARRATIVA AVANZADA: (1) FUTURO EN EL PASADO — 'was going to / was about to / would' para lo que iba a suceder. (2) PRESENTE HISTÓRICO — presente para pasado, crea inmediatez en storytelling informal. (3) DISCURSO INDIRECTO LIBRE — pensamientos del personaje sin verbo de reporte, manteniendo backshift. Técnica literaria de alto registro.
